before_code:
def mk2 (a b : ℕ+) : Fin 2 → ℕ+ := ![a, b]
def sigma1 (m : ℕ+) : ℕ := ...
description: VERY IMPORTANT: Avoid custom definitions where possible. Every def must be strongly justified and come with good API. Avoid small basic defs like sigma1 (use ArithmeticFunction.sigma) and mk2 (use ![a,b]). No custom notation when mathlib has one. Before creating ANY def, search mathlib exhaustively.

Each lemma/theorem must be separated by exactly one blank line. Fix ALL linter warnings: line length (100 char max), unused variables/hypotheses (prefix with _), unused simp args (remove from simp call). These are not optional.

before_code:
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
private lemma foo : ... := by ...
after_code:
private lemma foo : ... := by
  -- break proof into helpers or pass typeclass args explicitly
description: maxHeartbeats options MUST be removed. Either break the proof into smaller helpers so each fits in default heartbeats, or if the issue is typeclass inference, pass arguments explicitly to avoid search. set_option maxHeartbeats is never acceptable.
Consecutive rw calls MUST be combined into a single rw [a, b, c]. Same applies to simp_rw. This is a basic golfing rule that /cleanup must always apply.

before_code:
private lemma foo
    (a : Fin n → ℕ)
    (ha : ∀ i, 0 < a i)
    (hdiv : DivChain n a) :
after_code: private lemma foo (a : Fin n → ℕ) (ha : ∀ i, 0 < a i) (hdiv : DivChain n a) :
description: Maximize use of the 100-char line width for declaration signatures. Do NOT put one parameter per line when multiple fit. Merge continuation lines starting with 4-space-indent '(' into the preceding line whenever combined length ≤ 99 chars.

Do not use verbose coercions like (↑g : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℚ).det when g.det or g.val.det suffices. Let Lean infer the coercion. Only add explicit type annotations when Lean actually fails to elaborate without them.

before_code:
lemma mk2_pos (a d : ℕ) (ha : 0 < a) (hd : 0 < d) : ∀ i, 0 < (![a, d]) i := by
  intro i; fin_cases i <;> simp [*]

-- 91 call sites: mk2_pos a d ha hd
after_code:
-- DELETE the wrapper. At call sites use:
(fun i => by fin_cases i <;> simp [*])
description: NEVER create named wrapper lemmas for trivial 1-line proofs like positivity of ![a,d] or DivChain of ![a,d]. Inline the proof at every call site. The cleanup/decompose skills must check EVERY definition and ask: does this need to exist? If the body is ≤3 lines of standard tactics (fin_cases, simp, omega), delete it and inline.

before_code: Matrix.diagonal (fun m => ((![1, p] : Fin 2 → ℕ) m : ℤ))
description: For 2×2 diagonal matrices, use mathlib's matrix literal notation !![a, 0; 0, d] instead of building Matrix.diagonal from a vector with pointwise coercion. The expression Matrix.diagonal (fun m => ((![a, d] : Fin 2 → ℕ) m : ℤ)) is unreadable — use !![a, 0; 0, d] directly.

before_code: def T_ad (a d : ℕ) (ha : 0 < a) (hd : 0 < d) (h : a ∣ d) : HeckeAlgebra 2
after_code: def T_ad (a d : ℕ) (h : a ∣ d) : HeckeAlgebra 2 := if 0 < a ∧ 0 < d then ... else 0
description: DEFINITIONS should NOT carry hypotheses like positivity or primality. Handle edge cases internally (return 0). Only require hypotheses in LEMMAS where they are actually needed for the proof.

after_code: T[a,d] for T_ad, T[n] for T[1,n], ⟨n⟩ₕ for diamond operator T[n,n]
description: Add scoped notation for common Hecke operators: T[a,d], T[n] = T[1,n], and diamond operator notation for T[n,n]. Makes the API readable and matches standard mathematical notation.

before_code:
noncomputable def diagMat (a : Fin n → ℕ) (ha : ∀ i, 0 < a i) : GL (Fin n) ℚ :=
  GeneralLinearGroup.mkOfDetNeZero (Matrix.diagonal (fun i => (a i : ℚ))) (by ...)

def diagDet (a : Fin n → ℕ) : ℕ := ∏ i, a i

def diagMul (a b : Fin n → ℕ) : Fin n → ℕ := fun i => a i * b i
after_code:
-- DELETE all three. Use mathlib directly:
-- GeneralLinearGroup.mkOfDetNeZero (Matrix.diagonal ...) at call sites
-- ∏ i, a i  directly
-- fun i => a i * b i  directly
description: EVERY definition must be scrutinized during cleanup, not just proofs. A def that composes 2-3 mathlib functions (like diagMat = mkOfDetNeZero ∘ Matrix.diagonal, or diagDet = Finset.prod, or diagMul = pointwise mul) is a FORBIDDEN wrapper. Delete it and use mathlib directly at call sites. The cleanup/decompose skills MUST check definitions, not just proofs. This was missed across multiple cleanup passes because agents only looked at proof structure.

before_code: diagMat n a ha -- custom wrapper around Matrix.diagonal + mkOfDetNeZero
after_code: Matrix.diagonal (fun i => (a i : ℚ)) -- use mathlib directly, wrap in GL only where needed
description: Use Matrix.diagonal directly instead of custom diagMat wrapper. Only wrap in GeneralLinearGroup.mkOfDetNeZero where a GL element is actually needed. Add API lemmas for Matrix.diagonal (det, integrality, etc.) rather than wrapping in a custom def. This is the next major refactoring target.

Plan for Shimura Theorem 3.20 (R_p ≅ Z[X₁,...,Xₙ]). Proof by induction on n using: (1) weight induction for surjectivity via Prop 3.17 (T_diag_scalar_mul) and Lemma 3.19 (forgetful map ψ: R_p^(n+1) → R_p^(n)), (2) algebraic independence via ψ and non-zero-divisor property of T(p,...,p). Key missing: ψ homomorphism, Ker(ψ) characterization, weight induction step. See Shimura pages 59-60.

before_code:
# /cleanup Phase-4 worker template (current behavior)
# Audit items 5 (NAMING), 6 (LINE PACKING), 12 (STRUCTURE) report status as one of:
#   * applied at L<n>
#   * n/a: <reason>
#   * deferred for Phase 5
#
# Worker dispatched on `m6_2_extra_rep_levelRaise_bridge` (244-line body) reports:
#   12. STRUCTURE: 219 lines after golf; sub-helpers not extracted because
#       the signature is locked and downstream consumer needs this single closed form
# Worker dispatched on `miyake_4_6_5_iterated_helper_general`:
#   5. NAMING: existing convention preserved  -- did not rename, no objection logged
#   6. LINE PACKING: ok  -- did not pack, hypotheses stayed one-per-line
# Both runs report Gates: pass and main session accepts them.
# Result: bad names survive, long proofs survive, hypothesis blocks stay sparse.
after_code:
# /cleanup Phase-4 worker template (proposed)
# Items 5/6/12 become HARD GATES, not audit items:
#
# === HARD GATES (run REJECTED if any fail) ===
#   structure_gate:     body line count <= 60 (or <=15 for main theorems).
#                       Note: extracting `private` helpers ABOVE the declaration
#                       does NOT change the declaration's signature.
#                       "Signature locked" is not a valid reason to skip extraction.
#   naming_gate:        no forbidden patterns in declaration name:
#                       no `\d+_\d+_\d+_` (e.g. `4_6_5_`), no `m\d+_`,
#                       no `multipass_`, no `_aux\d+` numeric suffix.
#                       Rename per mathlib convention: describe what is PROVED.
#   line_packing_gate:  hypothesis lines packed to ~100 chars where possible.
#                       Multiple hypotheses per line is the norm, not the exception.
#
# === Audit-only items (report and address, deferral allowed) ===
#   1 (LINT), 3 (SET_OPTION), 4 (SIMP SQUEEZE), 7 (BY PLACEMENT), 8 (FORMAT),
#   9 (COMMENTS), 10 (DOCSTRING), 11 (VISIBILITY), 14 (JUNK DEF), 15 (EXISTS LEMMA),
#   16 (SHOW vs CHANGE), 17 (PUSH_NEG), 18 (GENERALISE)
#
# If body > 60 after worker run: REJECTED, re-dispatch with body line count as feedback.
# If declaration name still matches forbidden patterns: REJECTED, re-dispatch.
# If first hypothesis-rich line is below ~70 chars when next-token would fit:
#   REJECTED, re-dispatch with explicit pack instructions.
description: The /cleanup Phase-4 worker template has an escape valve that prevents quality enforcement on large files. Audit items 5 (NAMING), 6 (LINE PACKING), and 12 (STRUCTURE) are framed as report-or-defer, not pass-fail gates. On small files this is invisible (workers fix because there's little to defer); on large files (1000+ lines, many specialised declarations) workers routinely defer, leaving bad names, long proofs, and sparse hypothesis blocks in place. Specific failure modes observed: (1) 'signature locked' misread — workers refuse to extract `private` helpers ABOVE the declaration, claiming the signature-protection rule blocks it; the rule only blocks changing the declaration's OWN signature. (2) NAMING audit reports 'existing convention preserved' without ever proposing a rename, even when names encode internal scheme numbers (4_6_5_, m6_2_, multipass_) instead of mathematical content. (3) LINE PACKING reports 'ok' without packing one-per-line hypothesis blocks that could fit on shared lines up to 100 chars. The fix: convert items 5, 6, 12 to hard pass-fail gates with explicit rejection criteria. Re-dispatch workers whose runs violate any gate, feeding the violation back as the worker's prompt. Disambiguate 'signature locked' to clarify that helpers placed above the declaration are not signature changes. This was observed in a /cleanup run on an 8286-line modular forms file across ~15 sequential workers, where structure/naming/packing defects accumulated visibly while every worker reported 'Gates: pass'.

before_code: SMOObligations.lean (6462 L, 170 decls, single namespace HeckeRing.GL2)
after_code: SMOObligations.lean residual (435 L, route-B finale incl. strongMultiplicityOne_axiom_clean) imports a 7-module linear chain under SMOObligations/: Miyake465 (679), DescentCosets (1361), MiyakeDescend (888), LevelCommute (584), SquarefreeDecomp (1228), Lemma4_6_14 (1154), Lemma4_6_8 (297)
description: Split a 6.5K-line single-namespace file along Miyake §4.6 proof stages (4.6.5 -> descent cosets -> Hecke descend map -> level commute 4.6.6 -> squarefree decomp 4.6.7 -> 4.6.14 -> Main Lemma 4.6.8 -> route-B finale). Decls were already topologically ordered, so cutting at blank-line declaration boundaries in file order gave a pure linear import chain with zero forward references. 20 cross-module private helpers widened to non-private (removed leading 'private ' only); a Python cross-reference scan computed exactly which privates were used in a later module. Protected 197-line maxHeartbeats proof moved byte-identically (MD5-verified). Each module replicates the verbatim preamble (2 open lines + namespace + variable). Main theorem stayed axiom-clean [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

after_code: Newforms/{Basic 706, LevelRaiseComm 1367, MainLemma 720, CoeffSeq 1903, Fricke 2410, FrickeTwist 2567, MellinBridges 2463, BadPrimeAdjoint 1977, BadPrimeCosets 1747, BadPrimeReduction 2739} + residual Newforms.lean 984
description: Split a 19K-line newform-theory megafile into a 10-module linear import chain (Basic -> ... -> BadPrimeReduction) with the residual Newforms.lean re-exporting the chain and hosting the final SMO consumers. Cut points were all on `/-! ###` section markers so docstrings travelled with their declarations; the two `section CharSpaceDecomposition`/`section FrickeAdjoint` blocks were kept whole inside single modules. Each module replicates the full external import list + the file-scoped `noncomputable section` + `namespace`/`open`/`variable` preamble. Five private helpers used across module boundaries (shiftSL, shiftSL_mem_Gamma1, heckeT_p_all_levelRaise_comm_divN, Newform.HasHeckeT_p_divN_LRpd_in_cuspFormsOldExtended_proof, heckeT_n_levelRaise_comm) were widened to non-private. Key gotcha: a leading `noncomputable section` (before namespace) makes all defs noncomputable by default and MUST be replicated in every split module.
